title: sambucus neomexicana var. vestita
intro: Sambucus neomexicana var. vestita, also known as the New Mexico black elderberry, is a plant species that belongs to the Sambucus genus of flowering plants. The medicinal properties of Sambucus neomexicana var. vestita lie in its berries, which contain anthocyanins, flavonoids, and other bioactive compounds. The health benefits of this plant include its ability to support immune function, reduce inflammation, and exhibit antimicrobial and antiviral properties. Herbal preparations of Sambucus neomexicana var. vestita, such as teas, tinctures, and infusions, are used to promote overall health and well-being, particularly during the cold and flu season.
